_meta.note: La Liga MW38 overlays added — relegation/European stakes set

game_overlays.matchupNote: The Hurricanes look to seize a stranglehold on the Eastern Conference Final tonight, while the Canadiens fight to stave off elimination and keep their improbable postseason run alive. With a trip to the Stanley Cup Final on the line, Montreal’s desperation meets Carolina’s clinical precision in a high-stakes clash that will define the series trajectory.

The Knicks look to put a stranglehold on the Eastern Conference Finals tonight, while the Cavaliers face a desperate must-win scenario to keep their championship hopes alive. With New York holding a 2-0 series lead, Cleveland’s season effectively hangs in the balance as they return home to stave off an insurmountable deficit.
Girona needed all three points to keep their survival hopes alive — instead the 1-1 draw confirmed their relegation from La Liga. Elche survive; Girona go down on the final day of the season.
game_overlays.localNote: Relegation confirmed — Girona drop to the Segunda División after failing to win their final home game.

game_overlays.matchupNote: A must-win for Mallorca in their fight against the drop — the 3-0 victory improves their goal difference in what could be a knife-edge final-day tiebreaker for 18th place.
game_overlays.localNote: Mallorca vs Osasuna separated only by goal difference at 42 points each — La Liga's cruellest tiebreaker.

game_overlays.matchupNote: Osasuna fall to Getafe 1-0 on the final day while Mallorca win 3-0 — a four-goal swing in goal difference that could separate safety from relegation between two sides both finishing on 42 points.
game_overlays.localNote: The cruelest math in La Liga: Mallorca and Osasuna level on points, decided by the goals scored on the same afternoon.

game_overlays.matchupNote: Barcelona are already La Liga champions — but Valencia refused to give them a guard of honour, beating the title holders 3-1 in a brilliant final-day upset. The crowning is untouched; the scoreline is emphatic.
Real Madrid sign off the season with a 4-2 win over Athletic Bilbao — 86 points, eight behind Barcelona. A strong finish that doesn't change the title outcome but sets the tone for next season.
